ucs.title: [LLM] Cinnamon Tempest (BRONZE STARLIGHT) DLL side-loading of Cobalt Strike via signed binary in user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's signature post-exploit pattern: dropping a legitimately-signed third-party binary (e.g. vendor utilities like BrowserCore, vmnat.exe, dimss.exe, dgreen.exe, Sophos/McAfee/AnyDesk components) into a user-writable staging path and side-loading a malicious DLL (often named to match a real export — `OCI.dll`, `version.dll`, `wsc.dll`, `vmtools.dll`) that decrypts and runs Cobalt Strike. Cinnamon Tempest has used this chain repeatedly across LockFile, AtomSilo, Night Sky, Pandora, Rook and the BRONZE STARLIGHT-attributed ransomware families documented by Microsoft and Secureworks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Users\\*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("BrowserCore.exe","vmnat.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","dimss.exe","dgreen.exe","AnyDesk.exe","hpqAssetCopy_x64.exe","sc.exe","WerFaultSecure.exe","NotificationUI.exe","javaw.exe","DAU.exe","WhatsApp.exe") AND Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_paths values(Filesystem.file_name) as dll_names from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("OCI.dll","version.dll","wsc.dll","vmtools.dll","libcef.dll","loghours.dll","WINMM.dll","profapi.dll","DUI70.dll","BrowserCore.dll","sqlite3.dll","VERSION.dll","secur32.dll") AND Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Users\\*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | where like(dll_paths, replace(process_path, "\\\\[^\\\\]+$", "%")) OR like(process_path, replace(dll_paths, "\\\\[^\\\\]+$", "%")) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path parent_process_name dll_names dll_paths cmd
ucs.kql:
// Cinnamon Tempest DLL side-loading — signed-looking binary in staging path side-loading a DLL with the same parent folder
let StagingPaths = dynamic([@"\Users\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Intel\"]);
let AbusedHostBins = dynamic(["BrowserCore.exe","vmnat.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","dimss.exe","dgreen.exe","AnyDesk.exe","hpqAssetCopy_x64.exe","WerFaultSecure.exe","NotificationUI.exe","javaw.exe","DAU.exe","WhatsApp.exe","vfhost.exe","VsTskMgr.exe"]);
let TargetSideloadedDlls = dynamic(["OCI.dll","version.dll","wsc.dll","vmtools.dll","libcef.dll","loghours.dll","WINMM.dll","profapi.dll","DUI70.dll","BrowserCore.dll","sqlite3.dll","secur32.dll","NTDSAPI.dll"]);
let SuspectHosts =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ (AbusedHostBins)
    | where FolderPath has_any (StagingPaths)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              HostBinary = FileName, HostFolder = FolderPath,
              ParentBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256;
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (TargetSideloadedDlls)
| where FolderPath has_any (StagingPaths)
| join kind=inner SuspectHosts on DeviceId
| where datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, Timestamp1) between (-30 .. 600)
| extend HostFolderRoot = tolower(strcat(substring(HostFolder, 0, strlen(HostFolder) - strlen(tostring(split(HostFolder, @"\")[-1])))))
| where tolower(FolderPath) startswith HostFolderRoot   // DLL lives in the same folder as the signed binary = sideload
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, HostBinary, HostFolder,
          SideloadedDll = FileName, SideloadFolder = FolderPath,
          ParentBin, ParentCmd, HostBinarySHA256 = SHA256, DllSHA256 = SHA2561
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cinnamon Tempest DLL side-loading — Sentinel via SecurityEvent (4688) + ImFileEvent for the dropped DLL
let StagingPaths = dynamic([@"\Users\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Intel\"]);
let AbusedHostBins = dynamic(["BrowserCore.exe","vmnat.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","dimss.exe","dgreen.exe","AnyDesk.exe","hpqAssetCopy_x64.exe","WerFaultSecure.exe","NotificationUI.exe","javaw.exe","DAU.exe","WhatsApp.exe","vfhost.exe","VsTskMgr.exe"]);
let TargetSideloadedDlls = dynamic(["OCI.dll","version.dll","wsc.dll","vmtools.dll","libcef.dll","loghours.dll","WINMM.dll","profapi.dll","DUI70.dll","BrowserCore.dll","sqlite3.dll","secur32.dll"]);
let SuspectExec = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend BinName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])
    | where BinName in~ (AbusedHostBins)
    | where NewProcessName has_any (StagingPaths)
    | project ExecTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
| where TargetFileName in~ (TargetSideloadedDlls)
| where TargetFilePath has_any (StagingPaths)
| join kind=inner SuspectExec on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer
| where datetime_diff('minute', ExecTime, TimeGenerated) between (-30 .. 30)
| project TimeGenerated, ExecTime, Computer, Account,
          HostBinary = NewProcessName, HostBinaryCmd = CommandLine,
          DroppedDll = TargetFileName, DroppedDllPath = TargetFilePath,
          DllSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256, ActorParent = ParentProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Side-loading via abused legitimately-signed binaries dropped to user-writable paths is the central installer technique Microsoft and Secureworks attribute to BRONZE STARLIGHT / Cinnamon Tempest across LockFile, AtomSilo, Night Sky, Pandora, Cheers, and Rook campaigns. Pinning the host-binary list and the well-known target DLL names (OCI.dll, version.dll, wsc.dll, libcef.dll, vmtools.dll, BrowserCo

ucs.title: [LLM] Cinnamon Tempest GPO-driven ransomware fan-out (SYSVOL StartupScript write + multi-host shadow copy deletion)
ucs.description: Cinnamon Tempest characteristically uses Group Policy modification to mass-deploy Babuk-derived ransomware payloads (LockFile, Night Sky, Pandora, Cheers) across an enterprise rather than touching each endpoint individually. This UC chains an authored SYSVOL/Group Policy file write with the ransomware pre-encryption hallmark — vssadmin/wmic/PowerShell shadow copy deletion fanning out across many hosts within minutes — to catch the fan-out in flight, before mass file encryption begins.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as gpo_paths values(Filesystem.user) as gpo_writer from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path="*\\SYSVOL\\*" AND (Filesystem.file_name IN ("GptTmpl.inf","Registry.pol","ScheduledTasks.xml","Services.xml") OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Machine\\Scripts\\Startup\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Machine\\Preferences\\ScheduledTasks\\*") AND Filesystem.user!="*$" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename _time as gpo_time | join type=inner [| tstats summariesonly=true count as shadow_delete_hosts dc(Processes.dest) as host_count values(Processes.dest) as targets values(Processes.process) as shadow_cmds from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*shadows*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*" AND Processes.process="*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*Win32_Shadowcopy*" OR Processes.process="*Remove-WmiObject*Shadowcopy*")) by _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where host_count >= 3 | rename _time as shadow_time] | where gpo_time <= shadow_time AND (shadow_time - gpo_time) < 3600 | table gpo_time shadow_time dest user file_name gpo_paths host_count targets shadow_cmds
ucs.kql:
// Cinnamon Tempest GPO-driven ransomware fan-out
let GpoWrites =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FolderPath has @"\SYSVOL\"
    | where (FileName in~ ("GptTmpl.inf","Registry.pol","ScheduledTasks.xml","Services.xml","Groups.xml"))
         or FolderPath has @"\Machine\Scripts\Startup\"
         or FolderPath has @"\Machine\Preferences\ScheduledTasks\"
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("gpme.exe","mmc.exe","gpmc.msc","adaxes.exe")
    | project GpoTime = Timestamp, GpoActor = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              GpoSourceHost = DeviceName,
              GpoFile = FileName, GpoPath = FolderPath,
              GpoWriter = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              GpoWriterCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let ShadowDeleteFanout =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
         or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy","Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy"))
    | summarize ShadowDeleteFirst = min(Timestamp),
                ShadowDeleteLast  = max(Timestamp),
                ImpactedHosts     = dcount(DeviceId),
                HostList          = make_set(DeviceName, 200),
                CmdSamples        = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 50)
      by bin(Timestamp, 15m)
    | where ImpactedHosts >= 3;        // fan-out signal
GpoWrites
| join kind=inner ShadowDeleteFanout on $left.GpoTime <= $right.ShadowDeleteFirst
| where datetime_diff('minute', ShadowDeleteFirst, GpoTime) between (0 .. 60)
| project GpoTime, ShadowDeleteFirst, ShadowDeleteLast,
          GpoActor, GpoSourceHost, GpoFile, GpoPath, GpoWriter, GpoWriterCmd,
          ImpactedHosts, HostList, CmdSamples
| order by ShadowDeleteFirst desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cinnamon Tempest GPO-driven ransomware fan-out — Sentinel
let GpoWrites =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4663            // object access (file write)
    | where ObjectName has @"\SYSVOL\"
    | where ObjectName has_any ("GptTmpl.inf","Registry.pol","ScheduledTasks.xml","Services.xml","Groups.xml",@"\Machine\Scripts\Startup\",@"\Machine\Preferences\ScheduledTasks\")
    | where SubjectUserName !endswith "$"
    | project GpoTime = TimeGenerated, GpoActor = SubjectUserName,
              GpoSourceHost = Computer, GpoFile = ObjectName,
              GpoWriter = ProcessName;
let ShadowDeleteFanout =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where (BinName == "vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
         or (BinName == "wmic.exe"     and CommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
         or (BinName in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and CommandLine has_any ("Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy","Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy"))
    | summarize ShadowDeleteFirst = min(TimeGenerated),
                ShadowDeleteLast  = max(TimeGenerated),
                ImpactedHosts     = dcount(Computer),
                HostList          = make_set(Computer, 200),
                CmdSamples        = make_set(CommandLine, 50)
      by bin(TimeGenerated, 15m)
    | where ImpactedHosts >= 3;
GpoWrites
| join kind=inner ShadowDeleteFanout on $left.GpoTime <= $right.ShadowDeleteFirst
| where datetime_diff('minute', ShadowDeleteFirst, GpoTime) between (0 .. 60)
| project GpoTime, ShadowDeleteFirst, ShadowDeleteLast,
          GpoActor, GpoSourceHost, GpoFile, GpoWriter,
          ImpactedHosts, HostList, CmdSamples
| order by ShadowDeleteFirst desc
ucs.rationale: Cinnamon Tempest is one of the few ransomware operators publicly documented as preferring Group Policy modification — writing to SYSVOL Startup Scripts / Scheduled Tasks XML — to deploy their Babuk-derived encryptors organisation-wide rather than hands-on lateral movement per host. The chained signal of (a) a non-administrative actor writing to SYSVOL GPO containers and (b) shadow-copy deletion fa
